title: Auto-reconnect
author: AnarchystBR
body:
Please consider implementing an "Auto-reconnect" option for Wifinian.
It's pretty straight-forward: it would work by monitoring the connection status constantly, and if the connection is lost by any reason Wifinian would reconnect that wifi network automatically.

It would help people suffering from Windows' mysterious random wifi disconnections (including me), I've spent months now trying to solve this problem without any avail so far. Wifinian is helping me a lot because I can re-connect manually using its handy interface, but an "auto-reconnect" would minimize the headache immensely.

Thank you for your time.

title: Feature request: Always on top
author: clemone210
body:
Hello,
I think Wifinian is a great tool.
Would it be possible to have a feature to stay always on top?
This would come in handy!
Thx!

title: feature request: scan of better signal within same mesh network
author: gpmc01
body:
Is it possible to implement a functionality that is looking for the best signal/channel generated by various mesh routers within same WiFi network?
If I'm moving from one room to another with a better signal coverage, I'd like Wifinian to switch the connection to the mesh router with stronger signal.
Thank you!

title: feature request:  autoconnect before user login (service mode)
author: lovitus
body:
Hello dear dev.
the wonderful app could auto-connect wifi after user logged in . 
could it possible to auto-connect to wifi before log in ? after system restarted after power failure.

(my headless PC wont able to autoconnect to wifi when Lan is connected, that's why I use this app)

Have a nice day

title: Port it to C++
author: Zero3K
body: It would be nice if you ported it to C++ in order to reduce memory usage and get rid of the need for a user to install the currently mentioned version of .NET Framework.

title: Add display of wifi adapter speed and signal strength on the tray icon.
author: paolosezart
body:
This is a very convenient program for organizing connection to wifi networks.
I have a request to the developers. 
When you click on the Wifinian program icon in the tray appears a window with wifi networks and settings. In the rectangular, where it says “Connected” and the percentage signal strength, also still display the current connection speed of the WiFI adapter.
It would also be nice to add a percentage of signal strength to the icon in the tray.
And the third wish, when right-clicking on the tray icon, add besides the current options, the ability to disable auto-connection and auto-switching for all access points.
Thank you very much for a great program.

title: tooltip is showing the name of the app instead of the name of the current wifi connected
author: BabakNc
body: make the tooltip show the name of the current wifi connected

title: Custom settings
author: 1nfected
body:
I'm a little lost at how to use this.

What I'm after is to connect to X wifi network, when it's signal strength is say >= 80%, otherwise, connect to Y wifi network.

How do I achieve this with wifinian? I don't see any option to specify such settings anywhere.

title: Crash after long sleep
author: melaww
body:
Program often disappears from systray or crashes after waking up machine after standby/sleep overnight.

Can provide details on request.

title: Localization
author: KovalevArtem
body:
It is sad that the application is only in English - I propose to translate.
I am ready to help in the translation into Russian.
(For example, you can use Weblate, or any other service. It is completely free for open source projects.)

Here are examples of successful implementation:
https://github.com/xanderfrangos/twinkle-tray
https://hosted.weblate.org/projects/twinkle-tray/

https://github.com/Belphemur/SoundSwitch
https://hosted.weblate.org/projects/soundswitch
...

title: Status is not shown correctly with WPA3
author: Cai0407
body:
Windows 10 Pro 64bit (2004) with Intel AX200 (Wi-Fi 6 and WPA3 are available).

See the screenshot below.
![2020-09-27](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/2016274/94352830-ce41fe00-00a4-11eb-8911-a229a6c41edc.png)

1. "MAGI_X" is Wi-Fi 6, WPA3-Personal [AES], 5GHz. 100ch.
Wifinian says its status is "None [None]" and its signal is 0%, "Auto Connect" and "Auto Switch" are unavailable.

2. "MAGI" is Wi-Fi 6, WPA3/WPA2-Personal mix-mode [AES], 5GHz. 100ch.
Wifinian says its status is "WPA2-Personal [AES], 5GHz. 100".

In both cases, WPA3 is ignored.

title: Auto connect on startup
author: CP1832
body:
Hi emoacht:

I'm bothering you because each time I'm launching Wifinian for some reason, I have to manually check the Rush and Engage buttons. Is there any way to have any (or both) of these options checked automatically when starting Wifinian?

Thanks in advance.
